findings_addressed.description: Crash-safety bug: step 11 commits user_version=11 + EMPTY search_index in its OWN transaction; the reproject runs in a SEPARATE later transaction gated on crossing the step-11 boundary (before<11 && after>=11). A crash after step 11 commits but before the reproject commits leaves a durable v11 + empty index; the next open sees before==11, the boundary guard is FALSE, the reproject is skipped, and search_index stays empty forever -> recall silently collapses to ~0.
findings_addressed.how_fixed: Changed the reproject trigger from boundary-crossing to state/marker-based. A durable completion marker (_fathomdb_open_state['search_index_tokenizer_reproject_complete']='1') is written INSIDE the same BEGIN IMMEDIATE..COMMIT as the reindex DELETE+INSERT (atomic). open_locked now reprojects when schema_version_after>=11 AND the marker is ABSENT, instead of on the boundary crossing. A crash before the reindex commit rolls back both the index and the marker, so the next open re-runs (idempotent, crash-retryable). SCHEMA_VERSION unchanged (11); no new migration step (open-path repair logic, not a schema change).

fix_1.red_evidence: On GREEN-fix-absent code (boundary-gated), pr_g1_tokenizer_crash_recovery::ac_fts_tokenizer_reproject_recovers_after_crash FAILED with 'post-crash reopen recall 0.000 below the 0.9 floor'. The test builds a v11 corpus, then via a RAW rusqlite::Connection reconstructs the durable crash artifact (DELETE FROM search_index + clear the marker while keeping user_version=11) and re-opens the Engine; the boundary guard skips the reproject and the index stays empty (recall 0). After GREEN it passes (index repaired, recall back to floor).

blockers_encountered.description: agent-verify.sh STRICT=1 promotes the AC-037 netns-deny-egress check to a hard failure; `unshare -rUn` fails because rootless userns+netns is unavailable in this sandbox. This is a pre-existing environment/toolchain gap (no rootless user namespaces here), NOT a defect introduced by Slice 5. All code phases of agent-verify pass independently: agent-lint.sh rc=0, agent-typecheck.sh rc=0, agent-test.sh rc=0 (with FATHOMDB_SKIP_NETWORK_TESTS=1).
blockers_encountered.resolution: Not a code blocker — environment-only. Verified each agent-verify phase (lint, typecheck, test) green standalone; clippy clean; mkdocs build --strict green. No floor breach, no spec impossibility. Orchestrator/HITL can confirm AC-037 on a host with rootless userns.

additional_changes_made_in_scope: Code anchors had drifted from the prompt's witnessed line numbers (baseline 944cbb4 -> 8108bac): SearchResult derive @ lib.rs:819, struct @ 820, results @ 823; ReaderResponse alias @ 389; read_search_in_tx @ 3196; vector phase-1 @ ~3205-3253; FTS phase-2 @ ~3294-3308; dedup region @ ~3275; SCHEMA_VERSION @ schema lib.rs:6 (was 10); MIGRATIONS max step_id 10 @ ~251-254. Adapted per the adjust-on-divergence clause.

Plan-adjustment honored (recorded): took step_id 11 / SCHEMA_VERSION 10->11 for the tokenizer migration. The canonical-identity ADR still names step 11 / 10->11 for Slice 15 (now stale); did NOT touch Slice 15 — orchestrator renumbers Slice 15 to step 12 / 11->12.

Re-tokenization wiring (load-bearing fan-out): the step-11 drop+recreate leaves search_index EMPTY on a migrated DB, and database_has_pending_projection_work keys 'pending' off _fathomdb_projection_terminal (NOT search_index content), so the projection scheduler does NOT auto-repopulate. Added reproject_search_index_after_tokenizer_upgrade(connection) invoked from open_locked when the open crosses the step-11 boundary (schema_version_before<11 && after>=11), re-tokenizing search_index from canonical_nodes in a single writer-connection transaction before readers spawn. Projection-only; no source-record migration; idempotent. Added const SEARCH_INDEX_TOKENIZER_SCHEMA_VERSION=11.

Vector branch now also SELECTs vec_distance_l2 as the per-hit score (the prior candidate query discarded it); FTS branch SELECTs bm25(search_index). search_inner needed no logic change (the reader tuple's third element is now Vec<SearchHit>).

Consumer rework beyond the named harnesses: updated all .results readers to hit.body — cursors.rs, projection_runtime.rs (x3), excise_source.rs, fts5_injection_safety.rs, cursor_read_after_write.rs, perf_gates.rs (x2), perf_gates_devloop.rs, plus eu7_real_corpus_ac.rs:477 and eu8_ir_validation.rs:325. Migration-step assertions updated for the new step 11: fathomdb-schema/tests/migrations.rs (len 9->10, step_ids +11, x2) and fathomdb-engine/tests/compatibility.rs (step_ids +11). The Py/TS binding from_rust mappers map Vec<SearchHit>.

Added companion migration doc src/rust/crates/fathomdb-schema/migrations/011_search_index_tokenizer.sql carrying the accretion-exemption marker (matches steps 1-9 pattern; the migration linter scans these *.sql files). The inline Rust step 11 is the executable source.

Added a NEW migration accretion-guard note: the SQL uses CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE (not the 'CREATE TABLE' substring the guard/linter detect) plus DROP TABLE (names_removal=true), so the guard passes structurally; the exemption marker is carried for documentation/intent per the contract.

Py SDK: added PySearchHit pyclass + registration; added SearchHit dataclass in types.py + __all__/__init__ re-export + engine.py mapper + _fathomdb.pyi stubs. TS SDK: added SearchHit napi object + binding.ts NativeSearchHit + index.ts public SearchHit interface + search() mapper.
Promoted docs/guides to a nav SECTION in mkdocs.yml (Overview + Structured Search Hits) to wire the new guide page without orphaning nav.

fix-1 (codex P1 crash-safety): Marker mechanism — chose the atomic completion-marker over a state-gate (empty-index detection) because it is explicit, survives a legitimately-empty corpus (an empty search_index on a fully-reprojected empty-corpus DB would otherwise re-trigger a needless reproject forever), and reuses the existing _fathomdb_open_state key/value store (created in migration step 1, already home to projection_cursor). New const SEARCH_INDEX_TOKENIZER_REPROJECT_MARKER_KEY='search_index_tokenizer_reproject_complete'. reproject_search_index_after_tokenizer_upgrade now INSERTs the marker (value '1', ON CONFLICT upsert) inside the same transaction as DELETE+INSERT. open_locked guard changed from (before<11 && after>=11) to (after>=11 && !search_index_tokenizer_reproject_complete(conn)). New helper search_index_tokenizer_reproject_complete reads the marker; QueryReturnedNoRows => false (reproject needed), present='1' => true.

fix-1 follow-up: the marker-absence gate runs on EVERY v11 open, so synthetic/legacy DBs whose user_version was hand-stamped to 11 without running our migrations (compatibility.rs AC-048/AC-048b create only _fathomdb_embedder_profiles) lack the _fathomdb_open_state table; the marker read raised SqliteFailure 'no such table' and masked the downstream EmbedderIdentityMismatch / EmbedderDimensionMismatch. Fixed by treating a missing _fathomdb_open_state as 'reproject complete / nothing to do' (Ok(true)) so those DBs fall through to the embedder-identity probe unchanged. A genuinely migrated (crash-affected) DB always has the table (created step 1), so the crash-repair path is unaffected. compatibility.rs and pr_g1_* tests are read-only and were NOT edited; the code accommodates them.
agent_verify_result: PASS (code phases). agent-lint.sh rc=0; agent-typecheck.sh rc=0; agent-test.sh rc=0 (FATHOMDB_SKIP_NETWORK_TESTS=1: full cargo test workspace + pytest 68 passed/9 skipped + npm test 47 passed). cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets clean. cargo test -p fathomdb-engine and -p fathomdb-schema green. mkdocs build --strict green (0 errors/warnings, no orphaned nav). The ONLY non-pass is agent-verify.sh's STRICT=1 security step failing AC-037 netns-deny-egress (`unshare -rUn` unavailable — environment toolchain gap, not a Slice-5 defect; see blockers_encountered). RECALL FLOOR (>=0.90) HELD BEFORE AND AFTER the tokenizer change, MEASURED ACROSS THE MIGRATION: pr_g1_tokenizer_recall::ac_fts_tokenizer_floor_holds_across_migration builds a corpus on a v10 (unicode61) DB, measures recall, re-opens with the full migration set so step 11 runs (10->11), and re-measures on the SAME on-disk corpus. BEFORE (v10 unicode61) recall = 1.000; AFTER (v11 porter unicode61 remove_diacritics) recall = 1.000; delta = +0.000. Floor 0.90 satisfied at both schema versions; no breach. FIX-1 SELF-CHECK (codex P1): cargo test -p fathomdb-engine FULL suite green (incl. NEW pr_g1_tokenizer_crash_recovery + pr_g1_tokenizer_recall + pr_g1_search_hits + the previously-regressing compatibility AC-048/AC-048b now green); cargo test -p fathomdb-schema green; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets clean; pytest 67 passed/10 skipped (FATHOMDB_SKIP_NETWORK_TESTS=1); npm test 47 passed/0 failed. Recall floor STILL HELD before AND after migration with the marker change: pr_g1_tokenizer_recall BEFORE (v10) = 1.000, AFTER (v11) = 1.000, delta +0.000; crash-recovery test confirms recall recovers to >=0.90 (1.000) after reconstructing+repairing the durable crash artifact. docs untouched (no mkdocs run needed).
